competitive_position: GEP is the single strongest current threat as of Jul 2026—named as a shortlist finalist alongside Zycus in May 2026 and characterized as a 'strong threat', with no evidence of elimination or decline since. Coupa's status is ambiguous: on 1 Jul 2026 the buyer stated the S2C scope shift 'Put Zycus in stronger position vs Coupa', which may mean Coupa has been priced out or is no longer the lead threat for S2C, but it is not explicitly confirmed as eliminated. iValua was a 'strong threat' at May shortlist but has not been re-mentioned since, suggesting it may have faded or been eliminated (status uncertain, likely no longer active). SAP Ariba was part of the initial Jan 2026 RFP field but has not been mentioned since, indicating early elimination or fade. Recency-weighted ranking: GEP (high threat, active finalist per May 2026, no disqualifying evidence); Coupa (medium-to-low threat, may be faded/priced out per 1 Jul 2026 but not explicitly eliminated); iValua (low-to-dormant, shortlist finalist May 2026 but silent since); Ariba (dormant, named Jan 2026, no subsequent mention). The wedge: Zycus differentiates on full S2P suite integration with Merlin AI (Intake via MS Teams, AP Smartdesk OCR, agentic negotiation) vs competitors lacking these modules (per 2 Jul 2025 demo). Activation: make the S2C-focused revised pricing and phased implementation plan (requested 1 Jul 2026) sharper and more de-risked than GEP's, and ensure the RACI/project plan Zycus delivers ahead of the 13 Jul CFO meeting is more complete and convincing than what GEP provided, closing the deliverable gap the buyer flagged ('like all the other vendors gave them (we didn't)').

champion.source: Salesforce Contact Role (Evaluator); Avoma catch-up call 12 Dec 2025; Avoma demo call 2 Jul 2025; Avoma RFP briefing 12 Jan 2026; Next Step 30 Jun 2026 ('Tej Ram responded to Carl'); Next Step 1 Jul 2026; Avoma attendee lists (no CFO present); Engagement pulse (last contact 1 Jul 2026)
champion.summary: Tej Ram is an active operational champion and project lead, driving the RFP process and keeping Zycus informed, but his champion strength is 'developing' because he has not secured direct CFO (Economic Buyer) engagement for Zycus, has not introduced Zycus to the US parent stakeholders who will provide global sign-off, and has not yet locked commercial terms or demonstrated the ability to navigate the CFO and global approval process. Last contact 1 Jul 2026 (0 days ago, active engagement per pulse). The move is to ask Tej to arrange a direct CFO conversation immediately following the 13 Jul CFO scope meeting to convert operational advocacy into executive sponsorship and commercial validation.

items.quote: CFO is the confirmed budget authority and will coordinate global sign-off on costings, but has never appeared on any Zycus-recorded call or commercial discussion despite being 29 days from close on a $220k Best Case forecast deal.
items.source: Avoma attendee lists (all 7 touchpoints: no CFO present); Next Step 12 Dec 2025 and 12 Jan 2026 (CFO as EB confirmed); Next Step 1 Jul 2026 (CFO scope meeting week of 13 Jul is TTI-internal)

items.area: Vendor selection outcome and current RFP status
items.quote: March 2026 selection target is now 3+ months overdue as of 1 Jul 2026, and no update has been captured on whether selection has occurred, if delayed, or what the current status is. If selection occurred and Zycus was not chosen, the deal is lost.
items.source: Next Step 1 Jul 2026 (references completed, 'going through final stages', scope meeting 13 Jul, but no selection decision communicated); Avoma RFP briefing 12 Jan 2026 (March selection target)

items.quote: TTI explicitly flagged on 1 Jul 2026 that they want 'proper and revised RACI, project plan—like all the other vendors gave them (we didn't)', indicating Zycus has not delivered the implementation planning depth that competitors provided.

recommended_moves: Deliver revised S2C-focused RACI, project plan, and phased implementation costing by 10 Jul ahead of CFO scope meeting.
Request Tej arrange a direct CFO conversation immediately following the 13 Jul CFO scope meeting to validate budget, pricing, and contract timeline.
Ask Tej directly for vendor selection status and timeline, and whether the 13 Jul CFO meeting is the final gate before selection or if selection has already occurred internally.

Then if all good, Testing environment and deep dive/workshop into our Implementation strategy. 6th Jun Accelerated questions, and reference calls being set up (TataPlay, others) Duncan it more technical evaluator- Award date still looks to be in June 25th May: Provided additional information + References. Will review and do reference check, then next step will be sandbox testing, and detailed integration session. 11th May- confirmed by email- shortlisted to 2 for further implementation plans, commercials and hands on sandbox- need urgent and solid local reference. GEP, iValua strong threats here. 28th April: presenting solutions to senior management, then going through a shortlisting processes to 2-3 vendors (within the next 2 weeks) for further evaluations (potential use case demonstrations), then deciding on a vendor by 29th May. 10th April - Updated new timelines again. Decision now 29th May. 27th March: Confirmed new timeline - BAFO 24th April, Vendor Decision 8th May. 18th March: Received additional clarification questions to be completed and sent back by 23rd March. Mentioned the RFP timeframe has changed, will provide us more details shortly. 12th March: Completed onsite demo, they have a few more vendors to review. Will get back in touch once they have provided more indepth use cases to futher demonstrate. 5th March: Onsite presentation/demo for 3 hours scheduled for 10th March. 12th Feb: Submitted online questionnaires (350 questions) and sent Zycus proposal including pricing. Next step is demo/presentation with key stakeholders End of Feb/Early March. Aiming for this to be onsite. 10th Feb: Unable to meet CPO as it was conflict of interest during RFP process. Completion of RFP by the 14th Feb. 22nd Jan - Meeting CPO in Melbourne on 3rd Feb for an in-person meeting to gather further intelligence on their RFP process and where Zycus is currently sitting. 13th Jan Good position- Zycus seen as strong solution. ck to meet CPO next trip - solid Q1 opportunity- vs Coupa/iValua/Ariba 8th Jan: Received all RFP docs except pricing information will be sent on Monday 12th. Expectation to be submitted by 18th Jan. Also a quick briefing on the RFP with their internal stakeholders on Monday 12th Jan. As SC confirmed we are very good fit. 12th Dec - Had quick catch up with Rej, CFO requested to initiate RFP around 5th January, looking to decide on a vendor by April and start implementation. Will know more once I receive the RFP. 29th Nov - Will be releasing RFP in January to 3-4 vendors for S2P. 11th Nov - Invited to Webinar. Provided RFP template. He is currently working on creating a business case to provide to the board to get approval to start official evaluation. If approved will start evaluating in New Year. 5th Nov- email to set up connect sent. If no further actions required, move back to Initial interest for nuture Sep 11: Tej Ram was re-introduced to Luke Dougherty post Stanley's exit. He attended the EY Melbourne event. July 2: Conducted comprehensive demo to the team. They have requested for a hands on sandbox trial. Currently in process of gathering trial scenarios. June 23: Meeting details on June 18: Please see description below. TTi - Demo on July 2nd. Tej has given the scenarios that were clarified by Tanya. Jue 18, Demo on July 2 has been confirmed. We had an opportunity to clarify what Tej wants to look review. 16th June, 2025 Stanley and Tanya had an onsite catch up meeting with Tej Ram (Manager Procurement & Transformation). Demo scheduled for first week of July. RFP to be expected after a while 20th Jan 2025 Had a demo call with Tej Ram, Sonia Gopalan and Melissa Bartram. Will schedule more demos

sources: Avoma catch-up call 12 Dec 2025, Tej Ram: 'CFO requested to initiate RFP around 5th January'
Avoma RFP briefing 12 Jan 2026, Duncan Tennyson: 'CFO is based here in Melbourne... interacts with parent company in USA... global push... global sign-off needed on costings before contract'
Next Step 1 Jul 2026: 'They are meeting CFO week of 13th July to decided on scope'
Avoma attendee lists (all 7 touchpoints): no CFO or C-level TTI executive present
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: The CFO (Melbourne-based, name not disclosed) is the confirmed Economic Buyer and budget authority. On the 12 Dec 2025 call, Tej stated: 'CFO requested to initiate RFP around 5th January, looking to decide on a vendor by April and start implementation.' On the 12 Jan 2026 RFP briefing, Duncan clarified: 'CFO is based here in Melbourne... interacts with our parent company in USA quite a bit... there's probably a global push to get some decent systems for our company globally' and 'we will need to put a pause in business case... we'll go into selecting a preferred supplier and then going through the contract negotiation phase... global sign-off needed on costings before contract' (12 Jan 2026). The Next Step on 1 Jul 2026 states: 'They are meeting CFO week of 13th July to decided on scope' (Duncan and Tej meeting the CFO). However, the CFO has NEVER appeared on any Zycus-recorded call—no Avoma attendee list includes a CFO title or a C-level executive from the relevant stakeholder commercial and pricing conversations have been conducted through Tej (operational project lead) and Duncan (technical project manager), with no direct EB engagement captured. On a $220k Best Case forecast deal 29 days from close, the absence of EB engagement is a high-stakes gap: Zycus has no line of sight to the CFO's budget posture, pricing tolerance, decision criteria, or willingness to sign off on global costings. Champion Tej has translated CFO directives but has not secured direct CFO access for Zycus. The move is to request Tej arrange a direct CFO conversation ahead of or immediately following the 13 Jul scope meeting to validate budget, pricing, and contract timeline.
